title: Opentrons COVID-19 Testing Protocol for 24 Samples
author: Max Marrone
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.be48jgzw

steps.instruction: Clean the Station A OT-2. Cleaning an OT-2 COVID-19 Diagnostic Station by Max Marrone
Start pre-cooling the Temperature Module to 4 °C . This is used to actively cool the internal extraction control RNA that will be added to each sample.

Place the following labware on the OT-2's deck: Slot 1: An empty, sterile NEST 96 deep well plate. Slot 7: Temperature Module with Opentrons 96 aluminum block. Slot 8: A full, sterile rack of Opentrons 20 µL filter tips. Slot 9: A full, sterile rack of Opentrons 200 µL filter tips.

Prepare the sample collection tube rack. Each rack can hold 24 collection tubes. Be sure to observe best practices for safely handling the collection tubes full of patient sample. Empty tube rack Tube rack partially loaded with sample collection tubes
If needed, give the collection tubes a flick to make sure all the liquid has settled to the bottom. (Some liquid might be trapped against the swab scraper built into the tube.)
Uncap the collection tubes and place them in an Opentrons 24 tube rack. Sample 1 goes in position A1, sample 2 goes in position B1, and so on. Top to bottom, then left to right. %s %s

Prepare the reagent tube rack. In an Opentrons 24 2mL tube rack, place: Positions C1, C2, C3, D1, D2, D3: a sterile 1.5 mL tube containing 1.3 mL, lysis buffer , each. Position D6: a sterile 1.5 mL tube containing 580 µL, proteinase K . Remember to fold the tubes' caps back and secure them in their slots in the tube rack.

substeps.instruction: Prepare the reagent tube rack. In an Opentrons 24 2mL tube rack, place: Positions C1, C2, C3, D1, D2, D3: a sterile
mL, lysis buffer , each. Position D6: a sterile
mL tube containing 580 µL, proteinase K . Remember to fold the tubes' caps back and secure them in their slots in the tube rack.

steps.instruction: Ensure the Temperature Module has reached 4 °C .
Place a single generic 200 µL PCR tube containing 110 µL, internal extraction control RNA in well H1 (the bottom-left) of the Opentrons 96 aluminum block.
Place the reagent tube rack in slot 4.
Place the sample collection tube rack in slot 5.

Double-check all the labware to make sure it looks correct. Check that labware are inserted the right way around (well A1 at the top-left). Check that labware are properly clicked into the deck slots. Check that the tubes are seated flat in their tube racks.
Run the Station A protocol on the OT-2. The protocol file should be called V5_3-20_spike-StationA-24samples.py.

In the Run tab, click Start run . The OT-2 will home its motors and then begin the protocol. Do not click Start run more than once. A known bug will make the OT-2 run the protocol back-to-back. If you need to cancel the protocol for any reason, use the power switch to turn off the OT-2. When it turns back on, the pipettes will rise. If the pipettes had tips attached, you will need to manually remove them before starting again.
Wait for the run to finish. You can use this time to start preparing Station B.
Collect the NEST 96 deep well plate from slot 1. You will move it to Station B later.
Throw out the partially used tip racks and empty reagent tubes. Remove the used sample collection tubes.
Clean the Station B OT-2. Cleaning an OT-2 COVID-19 Diagnostic Station by Max Marrone

Place the following labware on the OT-2's deck: Slot 1: A Temperature Module with an Opentrons 96 well aluminum block and an empty, sterile NEST 100 µL PCR plate. Slot 3: A full, sterile rack of Opentrons 200 µL filter tips. Slot 4: A Magnetic Module with nothing on it. Slot 6: A full, sterile rack of Opentrons 200 µL filter tips. Slot 11: An empty NEST 1-well reservoir, for organic liquid waste.
Start pre-cooling the Temperature Module to 4 °C .
Prepare 3x 4.2 mL, IPA + magnetic beads :
In a sterile 15 mL Falcon tube, add 4 mL, isopropyl alcohol .
Vortex BP Genomics magnetic beads in their container for 20 seconds at high speed.
Add 200 µL, vortexed magnetic beads to the Falcon tube.
Repeat for a total of 3 Falcon tubes of IPA + magnetic beads. go to step #17
Wait for the Temperature Module to reach 4 °C .
Prepare reagent trough 1. In a sterile NEST 12 well reservoir, add: 6 mL, 70% ethanol to each of the wells 1—9 ( 54 mL, 70% ethanol total). 3 mL, nuclease-free water to well 11 .
Prepare reagent trough 2. In a separate, sterile NEST 12 well reservoir:
Add 6 mL, Wash Buffer 1 to each of the wells 10–12 ( 18 mL, Wash Buffer 1 total).
Vortex the tubes of IPA + magnetic beads.

For each of the wells 1–3, pipette an entire tube of 4.2 mL, IPA + magnetic beads into the well. This step is time-sensitive. As soon as you pipette the beads into well 1, they will start to settle. Continue quickly through the next steps.
Place the NEST 96 deep well plate that was output from Station A onto the Magnetic Module in slot 4 .
Place reagent reservoir 1 in slot 2.
Place reagent reservoir 2 in slot 5.

Double-check all the labware to make sure it looks correct. Check that labware are inserted the right way around (well A1 at the top-left for the plates, well 1 at the left for the troughs). Check that labware are properly clicked into the deck slots.
Run the Station B protocol on the OT-2. The protocol should be called StationB-24samples.py.

In the Run tab, click Start run . The OT-2 will home its motors and then begin the protocol. Do not click Start run more than once. A known bug will make the OT-2 run the protocol back-to-back. If you need to cancel the protocol for any reason, use the power switch to turn off the OT-2. When it turns back on, the pipettes will rise. If the pipettes had tips attached, you will need to manually remove them before starting again.
Wait for the run to finish.

The output is the NEST 100 µL PCR plate sitting on the aluminum block atop the Temperature Module in slot 1. When you're ready to move on to station C, collect the PCR plate. We've left the output on Station B for up to an hour before moving it to Station C.
